item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image, across multiple sources, comes from "Sandor H Szabo/EPA" with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons, and the second image comes from this source which is All Rights Reserved. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting idea. Badly written: Poorly worded, generally confusing as to how things work, and to how they’re being contained. Requires far more detail on containment procedures and the effects over “real” ravens; also, some origin story and description of the duplication process (Is it like mitosis? Is it instantaneous?). The possible paradoxes generated can be exploited to a much bigger degree.

Downvote. Not sure why it’s so highly rated, being quite disappointing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So three thoughts/questions.

1. So this SCP is basically uncontained?

2. "Any sightings of flocks related to SCP-1505 propagating or migrating are to be reported immediately."
But it's propagating, every 10 hours. And real ravens are possibly nearly extint, so effectively every flock of migrating ravens needs to be reported?

3. I can see why they would be concerned about other anomalous SCPs potentially "catching" this replicating effect. But given that the evidence is that 1505 kills other ravens, is there any indication that the effect is contagious?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so, it took me a bit to figure out the "direct kill" bit, but this is how I'm understanding it.

cough

Killing a specimen of SCP-1505 will destroy all the specimens that spawned from it, but not that particular specimen itself, because it was in turn spawned from another specimen?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Can't speak for everyone else here, but after reading this skip, I'd be pretty worried about the potential of raven's involved in other SCPs, such as SCP-2285 and SCP-2106 being infected with 1505. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Adding the following paragraph to the Containment Procedures:

Anomalies involving Corvus corax specimen, including SCP-2285 and SCP-2106, are to be monitored for potential contamination by SCP-1505. Neutralization protocols are in place in the event SCP-1505's effect manifests in these or other anomalies.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's like Gods NRDC

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as in, if the influenza virus is out of the raven, it can still respawn on it's own?

Well, I'm glad this is fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Say, you have a raven that duplicates into two. The original respawns back where it was duplicated and performs all the exact actions as before, including duplicating and respawning over and over. The two duplicates, then go on to do things until they too duplicate and respawn. So this exponential growth can get out of hand pretty quickly when not addressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm aware that this question is coming a pretty significant period of time after the SCP was posted, but the actual mechanic of duplication of the time loop is sticking me. The wording in the article is really confusing me. Is there any way someone could lay it out in such a way that even an idiot could understand it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I live temporal things. This is weird/creepy strange enough for me to upvote.

Is the implication that normal ravens are so hard to find because they've all been killed/replaced?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I might have really missed something here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All specimens of SCP-1505 also appear to be carriers of the avian influenza virus.

What if the virus has the same anomaly?

>implications

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, no vote. Either I'm missing part of this, or it's not in the article. Idea is workable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god I replaced the rain texture with ravens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A "raven paradox", huh? I guess it's not bad, but I'm withholding my vote until it has something that grabs my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hey, we don't allow your kind in here!"

"Why not? I'm a fun guy!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think there are still some minor tonal issues ("extremely cramped box" jumps out at me), but this is a neat concept. Also,

Any reports… are to be reported immediately.

The ravens aren't the only thing stuck in a time loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing special, but good all around. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one. It's a nice twist on the "infinitely self replicating thing" thing.

The only qualm I have with is the word "respawn", and I think that's just because of how much I associate it with video games (which changes the tone of the article quite a bit for me). I can't think of any other decent word that could describe it.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A flock of repeating birds. I love it.

Although the term 'fungal agent' immediately made me create 'Agent Barry Mushroom'.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an infinite source of fertilizer and the Foundation doesn't seem to know or care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find myself upvoting, partly because one of my somewhat silly "kill the world" thoughts, specifically thought up as a way to troll Novikov's self-consistency principle would be to simply duplicate an object into a specific point of time until there's enough mass to form a black hole big enough, which'd probably force a time paradox since there's nothing else it can do, unlike actions which can fail - enough mass in one place will collapse onto itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the tone of the note makes the difference. What made said difference obvious is the use of the word "fucking". Still getting an upvote, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't think of anything the article would lose without it, honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honestly really close to just taking the note down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That 05 note is the most ridiculous thing I've read in an otherwise-not-godawful SCP all week. The whole thing is pretty ridiculous, frankly.

But I kinda like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My head-canon is that this O5 is a certain Chinese revolutionary..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No real disruptions, I indeed thank you for the quick edit.

Upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I cleaned up the tone a bit, does it still add anything or just serves as a huge distraction for you guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, the note from O5 feels a little bit "lolfoundation".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That letter from the O5 is painful.

So nobody is going to get reprimanded if they accidentally shoot a real raven

I don't even.

EDIT: Now that the letter is gone, I'm removing my downvote. I still don't care enough about this to upvote it, though.
